raw_consumer_statement:
A charge of XXXX was made to my Citi Advantage/Master card on XX/XX/XXXX. I immediately called the credit card company to dispute the charge. I heard from the credit card company by phone and they asked some banal questions that I had already answered. Shortly after this call I received an impersonal letter stating that the case was closed because the card was present when the service was paid for and it would reappear on my next statement. I understood the charge was for an auto repair shop. I called the credit card company to question why it was closed and the representative said, " no, the case is still opened. '' I received my current statement today, XX/XX/XXXX, and the charge, plus an interest charge is still on my bill. 
I called to speak to a representative and then I was transferred to the Fraud department. The representative said she was sorry but since my card was present, the charge would remain and there was nothing they could do. 

I called back the Fraud department and asked to speak to a Supervisor. I told her I wanted the name of the company, their address and phone number. The statement only had XXXX as the address and when I googled the name XXXX XXXX, what came up was a hotel in XXXX. There was no name, or phone number for an auto repair shop. This city is XXXX  hours from where I was staying in XXXX. The card was with me the whole time and I can't explain how they could use my card. It appears that the investigation done was only to check whether the chip on my card was used. No other information re the name or address of the service provider or merchant was available at the credit card company. They did a very superficial investigation and have not been very helpful.
